---
title: "Reproducible science: open-source code, datasets, and sharing practices"
root: ".."
---

::: {.callout-note}
This page is a **scaffold**. Replace placeholder text with your own synthesis and citations.
:::

## Why reproducibility is part of “diversity”

*Placeholder:* Discuss why openness and reproducibility are linked to inclusion:
- If only a subset of groups can access tools/data, results may encode bias
- Reproducible workflows enable auditing for fairness and generalisability
- Shared code and transparent reporting allow error correction and community benefit

## Open-source code and dataset sharing

*Placeholder:* Discuss:
- Data availability statements (DAS)
- Code availability statements (CAS)
- Reproducibility checklists and badges
- Incentives and barriers

## Reproducibility initiatives

*Placeholder:* Mention community initiatives relevant to neuroimaging/omics/open science.

## Your project’s reproducibility study

One of the project deliverables is a reproducible repository:

> **Investigating the proportion of papers sharing code and datasets in the _Alzheimer’s & Dementia_ journal**

Once created, link it from the **Original research** page.
